skill.name: non-tumor-ml-research-planner
skill.description: Generates structured, publication-oriented research plans for non-tumor biomedical machine learning studies. Covers metabolic, cardiovascular, kidney, inflammatory, autoimmune, infectious, neurological, endocrine, and wound healing diseases. Designs diagnostic biomarker studies combining GEO datasets with feature selection and ML modeling across four workload tiers: Lite, Standard, Advanced, and Publication+.
skill.summary: Generates complete non-tumor biomedical ML research designs from a user-provided research direction
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 48 static analysis findings were evaluated and confirmed as false positives. The 40 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings referred to ML method names (RF=Random Forest, LASSO=Least Absolute Shrinkage) in bioinformatics research documents, not cryptographic code. The 6 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings were standard markdown inline code formatting for file references. The 3 'System reconnaissance' and 3 'Network reconnaissance' findings were research methodology terminology in validation strategy sections. The 2 'Hardcoded URL' findings were a legitimate GitHub source reference. This skill is a pure markdown instruction guide with no executable code, network connections, or data access. No prompt injection or malicious intent was detected.

content.user_title: Plan non-tumor ML research studies for bioinformatics papers
content.value_statement: Researchers struggle to design rigorous non-tumor bioinformatics papers with proper machine learning validation. This skill generates complete, publication-ready research plans with tiered workload configurations and reviewer-risk mitigation.
actual_capabilities: Generate complete research plans for non-tumor ML bioinformatics studies across four workload tiers
Select optimal study patterns based on disease, mechanism theme, and research goals
Provide step-by-step workflows including DEG analysis, feature selection, and model validation
Include reviewer-risk analysis with specific mitigations for each design
Produce dataset validation strategies with external cohort recommendations
limitations: Does not execute code, access GEO databases, or perform any data analysis
Cannot verify dataset availability or quality for specific diseases
Research plans require manual execution by a qualified bioinformatics researcher
Does not cover tumor or oncology studies

use_cases.title: First bioinformatics paper
use_cases.description: A graduate student needs a structured plan for their first non-tumor ML diagnostic biomarker paper with clear steps and validation strategy.

use_cases.title: Clinical ML study design
use_cases.description: A clinical researcher wants to combine GEO transcriptomic datasets with machine learning to build a diagnostic model for a metabolic disease.

use_cases.title: Publication-ready advanced plan
use_cases.description: An experienced bioinformatician plans a multi-dataset consensus study with immune infiltration analysis and regulatory network interpretation for a high-impact journal.

prompt_templates.title: Basic biomarker study plan
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to study [disease name] using machine learning. Help me design a bioinformatics paper with GEO datasets, DEG analysis, and a diagnostic model.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user starting a standard non-tumor ML study

prompt_templates.title: Mechanism-restricted study
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a [pyroptosis/ferroptosis/autophagy/senescence]-related ML study for [disease name]. Include mechanism gene set intersection, feature selection, and immune infiltration analysis.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user with a specific biological mechanism focus

prompt_templates.title: Multi-dataset consensus plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-dataset consensus ML study for [disease name] using 2-3 GEO cohorts. Include Venn overlap analysis, training-validation split, external validation, and robustness checks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user seeking stronger reproducibility claims

prompt_templates.title: Full publication+ design
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Publication+ tier research plan for [disease name] with multi-cohort consensus, ensemble modeling, immune infiltration, regulatory networks, and reviewer risk pre-emption. Include a publication upgrade path.

output_examples.input: I want to study diabetic foot ulcer using machine learning. Help me design a bioinformatics paper.
output_examples.output:
Core research question: Can transcriptomic profiling combined with ML identify diagnostic biomarkers for diabetic foot ulcer?

Specific aims: (1) Identify DEGs between DFU and normal tissue, (2) Build RF + LASSO diagnostic model, (3) Validate in external cohort

Four-tier configuration table with workload estimates, figure counts, and strengths/weaknesses for Lite through Publication+ tiers.

Recommended primary plan: Standard tier with two GEO datasets, DEG analysis, RF + LASSO feature selection, multivariate logistic model, ROC + calibration + DCA, and ssGSEA immune infiltration.

output_examples.input: Plan a pyroptosis-related ML study for diabetic nephropathy.
output_examples.output:
Research design: Mechanism-restricted ML study intersecting pyroptosis gene set with diabetic nephropathy DEGs.

Gene set source: 33 pyroptosis-related genes from literature and GeneCards.

Workflow: GEO dataset selection, DEG analysis, pyroptosis gene intersection, GO/KEGG enrichment, RF + LASSO feature selection, multivariate logistic model, ROC + calibration + DCA, ssGSEA immune infiltration, and reviewer risk mitigation.

Key outputs: Prognostic gene signature, diagnostic nomogram, immune correlation analysis, and 8-12 publication-ready figures.

best_practices: Always specify the disease name and research goal for optimal plan generation
Mention mechanism themes such as pyroptosis or ferroptosis for focused gene set analysis
Identify potential GEO datasets early to confirm study feasibility before committing to a tier
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for tumor or oncology studies, use a dedicated oncology skill instead
Avoid requesting a plan without providing a specific disease or phenotype
Do not skip the reviewer risk section when presenting final plans to users

faq.question: What diseases does this skill cover?
faq.answer: Non-tumor diseases including metabolic, cardiovascular, kidney, inflammatory, autoimmune, infectious, neurological, endocrine, and wound healing conditions. It does not cover cancer or oncology studies.

faq.question: What is a mechanism theme?
faq.answer: A biological process such as pyroptosis, ferroptosis, autophagy, senescence, or mitophagy. Providing a mechanism theme allows the skill to generate a more focused and biologically anchored research plan.

faq.question: Can this skill run machine learning models?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates research plans and workflows only. All data analysis and model building must be performed manually using tools like R, Python, or bioinformatics platforms.

faq.question: What is the difference between workload tiers?
faq.answer: Lite (2-4 weeks, minimal analysis), Standard (4-8 weeks, publication-ready default), Advanced (8-14 weeks, enhanced robustness), and Publication+ (14+ weeks, multi-module high-impact design).

faq.question: How do I find GEO datasets for my disease?
faq.answer: The skill includes a dataset search guide with GEO query strategies and MeSH terms. It can recommend relevant GSE series types for your specific disease when dataset availability is uncertain.

faq.question: Can this skill help with already collected data?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide your dataset details and study goals, and the skill will design a workflow for your specific data including appropriate validation strategies and tier recommendations.
